title: Health Insurance Cashless Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Identify Network Hospital: Visit insurer's app/website or call helpline to find a network hospital. For planned treatments, check network at least 2 weeks in advance. Tip: Download the insurer app and save TPA helpline in phone contacts.

Step 2 — Inform TPA/Insurer: For planned hospitalization, inform TPA at least 72 hours in advance. For emergencies, inform within 24 hours of admission. Provide: policy number, member ID, patient name, treating doctor, diagnosis, and estimated treatment cost.

Step 3 — Cashless Authorization Request: Hospital insurance desk will fill the pre-authorization form on your behalf. TPA reviews and responds within 1 hour (planned) or 30 minutes (emergency) as per IRDAI mandate. Authorization mentions the approved amount.

Step 4 — Treatment: Undergo treatment at the network hospital. The cashless authorization covers the approved medical expenses. Personal consumables (toiletries, TV, phone charges, attendant food) and non-medical items are not covered — pay these separately.

Step 5 — Final Bill Review at Discharge: Before discharge, review the final hospital bill. Hospital sends final bill to TPA. TPA may take 1–3 hours to settle after discharge. You pay only the non-covered amounts (copay, deductible, non-medical items).
Step 6 — Get Discharge Summary: Collect discharge summary, investigation reports, and prescription. Even for cashless, keep all original documents — needed if any future claim dispute arises.
documents_needed: Referral letter (if referred by another doctor)
timeline_cashless_auth: 1 hour (planned), 30 minutes (emergency) for initial pre-auth. Final settlement: 3 hours post-discharge.
tpa: Depends on insurer. Common TPAs: Medi Assist, Vidal Health, MD India, Heritage Health. Some insurers (Star Health, Care Health) have in-house TPA.
toll_free: Star Health: 1800-425-2255. HDFC ERGO: 1800-2666. Care Health: 1800-102-4488. Bajaj Allianz: 1800-209-0144. New India: 1800-209-1415
common_rejection_reasons: PED waiting period not completed — disease excluded during waiting period
Pre-authorization not taken — patient walked in without informing TPA
Treatment not covered under policy — cosmetic, dental, vision, self-inflicted injury
Documents incomplete — missing investigation reports or discharge summary
Room rent exceeded policy sublimit — proportionate deduction applied
escalation_path: Hospital Insurance Desk → TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman → Consumer Forum

title: Health Insurance Reimbursement Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Get Treatment: Undergo treatment at any hospital (network or non-network). Pay all bills from your pocket. Keep ALL original documents — do not lose them.

Step 2 — Intimate Claim: Intimate your insurer/TPA about the hospitalization within 24 hours of emergency admission or 72 hours of planned admission. Even for reimbursement, timely intimation is mandatory — late intimation can lead to rejection.

Step 3 — Collect Documents: Before leaving hospital, collect: discharge summary, final itemized bill, all investigation/lab reports, prescription for medicines purchased outside, payment receipts for all amounts paid.

Step 4 — Download Claim Form: Download the reimbursement claim form from insurer's website or collect from their branch. Fill completely — attach all supporting documents as per checklist in the form.

Step 5 — Submit Claim: Submit within 15–30 days of discharge (check your policy for exact timeline). Submit via: online portal upload, insurer's app, courier to insurer/TPA office, or drop at branch. Get acknowledgment with claim number.

Step 6 — Claim Processing: Insurer/TPA scrutinizes documents. They may request additional documents within 7 days of claim receipt. Respond within the stipulated time (usually 15 days) — non-response leads to claim closure.

Step 7 — Settlement: Valid claims must be settled within 30 days of receiving complete documents (IRDAI mandate). Amount is credited to registered bank account. If rejected, written reasons must be provided.
documents_needed: Original payment receipts for all amounts paid
All investigation reports (blood tests, X-ray, MRI, CT scan, etc.)
Original pharmacy bills (for medicines purchased outside hospital)
Pre-hospitalization documents (3 months prior): consultation bills, prescription, diagnostic reports
Post-hospitalization documents (60–90 days): follow-up consultation bills and prescriptions
Cancelled cheque or bank account details for NEFT
reimbursement_timeline: 30 days from receipt of complete documents (IRDAI mandate). Pre-hospitalization: 30 days before admission. Post-hospitalization: 60–90 days after discharge (varies by policy).
tpa: Submit to TPA if your insurer uses TPA, or directly to insurer
toll_free: Star Health: 1800-425-2255. HDFC ERGO: 1800-2666. Care Health: 1800-102-4488. Bajaj Allianz: 1800-209-0144
common_rejection_reasons: Claim submitted after deadline (15–30 days post-discharge)
Documents missing — original bills not submitted, only photocopies
Treatment not covered (dental, vision, cosmetic, self-inflicted)
Hospital not recognized/registered (not in insurer's system)
Pre-hospitalization/post-hospitalization period exceeded (claim for consultation 4 months later)
escalation_path: TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance Cell (30-day resolution mandate) → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman → Consumer Forum → Civil Court

title: Life Insurance Death Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Intimate the Insurer: Nominee informs the life insurer of the death as soon as possible. Call insurer's helpline or visit nearest branch. Provide: policy number, name of deceased, date of death, cause of death.
Step 2 — Collect Claim Form: Download or collect life insurance death claim form from insurer. Fill in details of deceased, nominee, and circumstances of death.
Step 3 — Gather Documents: Compile all required documents (see document list). Certified copies are usually acceptable but some insurers require originals for specific documents.
Step 4 — Submit Claim: Submit at insurer's branch or through online portal. Get acknowledgment with claim reference number. Insurer should acknowledge within 3 working days.

Step 5 — Investigation (if required): Insurer may investigate if: death within 3 years of policy inception; suspicious circumstances; claim amount is large. Investigation should be completed within 90 days.
Step 6 — Settlement: IRDAI mandates settlement within 30 days (non-investigated claims) or within 120 days (investigated claims) from date of claim registration. Nominee's bank account is credited.
Step 7 — Get Settlement Letter: Insurer provides settlement letter confirming final paid amount. Keep for tax records (death benefit is tax-free — but document helps prove the receipt).
documents_needed: Original policy document (or indemnity bond if lost)
Death certificate (original or certified copy from municipality/gram panchayat)
Medical cause of death certificate (from attending doctor)
Post-mortem report (if death was accidental or sudden — required by insurer for investigation)
FIR and police report (if accidental death)
Hospital records/discharge summary (if death in hospital)
Nominee's bank account details and cancelled cheque
Age proof of deceased (if not already submitted at policy inception)
Certificate of employment (for employer-sponsored group policies)
reimbursement_timeline: 30 days for non-investigated claims, 120 days for investigated claims from claim registration date
tpa: Life insurers usually process death claims in-house. No TPA involved.
common_rejection_reasons: Suicide within first year of policy (exclusion in most policies; second year onwards may be covered)
Non-disclosure of pre-existing condition at proposal stage (misrepresentation)
Policy lapsed at time of death — premiums not paid
Death due to excluded cause (war, nuclear risk, self-inflicted injury)
Claim filed after 3 years of death — delay without valid reason
Nominee not registered or multiple nominees with disputes
escalation_path: Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Life Insurance Ombudsman → Civil Court

title: Life Insurance Maturity/Survival Benefit Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Insurer Pre-notification: Most insurers send a maturity intimation 2–3 months before maturity date by post, email, or SMS. If not received, contact insurer 3 months before maturity with your policy number.
Step 2 — Update Contact Details: Ensure your current address, mobile, email, and bank account details are updated with the insurer well before maturity. Incorrect bank details delay payment.
Step 3 — Collect Discharge Voucher: Insurer sends a discharge voucher (receipt form for accepting maturity proceeds). Sign and return this form with required documents.
Step 4 — Submit Documents: Submit to insurer's branch or via online portal. For amounts above ₹50,000, original policy document must be surrendered.

Step 5 — Tax Assessment: Check if maturity is taxable. SA/premium ratio ≥ 10× → tax-free under 10(10D). SA/premium < 10× → taxable; insurer deducts 5% TDS on gain. If income is below taxable limit, submit Form 15G (below 60) or 15H (above 60) to avoid TDS.
Step 6 — Receive Payment: Payment credited to your registered bank account within 5–15 working days of document submission. For large amounts, may take up to 30 days.
Step 7 — Update Records: Keep settlement letter for tax filing. Check Annual Information Statement (AIS) on IT portal to ensure correct reporting of maturity proceeds.
documents_needed: Bank account details and cancelled cheque
Form 15G or 15H (if income below taxable limit and maturity is taxable)
reimbursement_timeline: 15–30 days from receipt of complete documents
common_rejection_reasons: Bank account details mismatch — wrong IFSC or account number
Policy document not surrendered (for large amounts)
KYC not updated — Aadhaar-PAN not linked
Address mismatch causing courier return of cheque (for manual payments)
escalation_path: Insurer Customer Service → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Motor Insurance Own Damage (OD) Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Ensure Safety First: After an accident, ensure your safety and others'. Call 108 (ambulance) if there are injuries. Do NOT move the vehicle unless safety requires it.

Step 2 — Document the Scene: Take photos/videos of vehicle damage, accident site, third-party vehicle (if any), road conditions, and any signboards/landmarks. Time-stamped photos strengthen the claim.
Step 3 — FIR (if required): FIR is mandatory for: theft, major accidents, third-party injury/death. For minor accidents with no injury, FIR is not mandatory but recommended.

Step 4 — Intimate Insurer Immediately: Call insurer's helpline within 24 hours. Register claim and get claim number. Surveyor will be assigned. Do NOT get vehicle repaired before surveyor inspection (unless emergency).

Step 5 — Surveyor Inspection: Insurer's appointed surveyor inspects the vehicle damage. Cooperate fully — the survey report determines claim amount. Surveyor should inspect within 24–48 hours for non-cashless cases.

Step 6 — Cashless Repair (if at Network Garage): Take vehicle to insurer's cashless garage. Garage sends repair estimate to insurer. Insurer approves estimate. Repairs done — you pay only deductible and non-covered items.
Step 7 — Reimbursement (if at Non-Network Garage): Get repairs done after surveyor's inspection. Collect all bills. Submit to insurer within 7 days of repair completion.
Step 8 — Claim Settlement: Insurer settles within 30 days of survey completion and document submission.
documents_needed: Photos of damage at accident site
timeline_cashless_auth: 24–48 hours for surveyor visit. Cashless repair started after insurer approves estimate.
reimbursement_timeline: 30 days from receipt of surveyor report and complete documents
tpa: Motor claims handled directly by insurer. No TPA in motor.
toll_free: Bajaj Allianz Motor: 1800-209-5858. HDFC ERGO Motor: 1800-2666. Tata AIG Motor: 1800-266-7780. ICICI Lombard: 1800-2666. New India Assurance: 1800-209-1415
common_rejection_reasons: Driving license invalid or expired at time of accident
Drunk driving (DUI) — excluded from all motor policies
Vehicle RC expired (not valid at time of claim)
Damage not consistent with accident description
Policy lapsed at time of accident
Damage from outside policy territory (driving in Nepal without endorsement)
Wear and tear damage — gradual deterioration is excluded
escalation_path: Insurer Surveyor → Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman → Consumer Court → Civil Court

title: Motor Third-Party Claim Process
process: Step 1 — FIR is Mandatory: For any third-party claim (injury, death, property damage), file FIR with police immediately. Third-party claims go to Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) — FIR is required.
Step 2 — Exchange Information: Exchange name, address, vehicle number, insurance policy details, and driving license details with the other party involved.
Step 3 — Intimate Insurer (Both Parties): The at-fault vehicle owner's insurer is liable. Intimate your insurer within 24 hours. If you are the claimant (third party), you need to file at MACT.

Step 4 — MACT Filing (for Claimants): File petition at Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (district court level). Claimant (injured/kin) files for compensation. No court fee. The at-fault driver's insurer pays compensation as awarded by Tribunal.
Step 5 — Investigation: Insurer investigates liability (who was at fault). Police charge sheet, FIR, eye-witness statements, and accident reconstruction may be used.

Step 6 — Settlement or Tribunal Award: Claim can be settled through negotiation or Tribunal award. Structured formula (as per Supreme Court guidelines) for compensation in death/disability cases. Property damage capped at ₹7.5 lakh.
Step 7 — Payment: Tribunal award must be paid within 30 days by insurer. Delay invites interest penalty.
documents_needed: Driving license of at-fault vehicle's driver
Medical bills (if injured — keep all originals)
Death certificate and post-mortem report (if death)
Income proof of deceased (for compensation calculation)
MACT petition (drafted by lawyer, file in MACT court)
reimbursement_timeline: MACT proceedings typically take 1–3 years for a decision. Emergency interim relief may be ordered by MACT within weeks.
tpa: N/A — handled by insurer's legal team and MACT
common_rejection_reasons: Driver was drunk (DUI) — insurer may deny TP claim as well
Vehicle used commercially without commercial insurance
Hit-and-run — MACT has Solatium Fund for hit-and-run victims
License invalid/expired — insurer may contest liability
escalation_path: MACT → High Court (appeal) → Supreme Court (final appeal). Insurance disputes: IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman (for insurer procedural issues, not MACT awards)

title: Critical Illness Insurance Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Diagnosis: Get diagnosed with a covered critical illness by a qualified specialist. The diagnosis must meet the policy's specific definition (refer to standardized IRDAI definitions).

Step 2 — Survival Period: Survive the defined survival period (usually 30 days from diagnosis). Many policies require surviving 30 days after diagnosis to confirm the claim. Some insurers have waived the survival period post-2022.

Step 3 — Intimate Insurer: Contact insurer within the claim intimation timeline stated in policy (usually 7–15 days of diagnosis). Provide preliminary information: policy number, diagnosis, name of treating hospital/doctor.

Step 4 — Collect Documents: Gather all medical evidence proving the critical illness has occurred as per policy definition. For cancer: biopsy/pathology report and oncologist's report. For heart attack: ECG and cardiac enzyme tests. For stroke: MRI/CT scan report.
Step 5 — Submit Claim: Fill claim form and submit with all medical documents to insurer. Get acknowledgment.
Step 6 — Medical Review: Insurer may appoint an independent medical examiner (IME) to review documents or examine the patient. IME report is shared with claimant.
Step 7 — Lump Sum Payment: On approval, lump sum benefit amount is paid directly to policyholder's bank account. No bills required — this is a defined benefit, not reimbursement.
documents_needed: Diagnosis report (biopsy report for cancer, ECG for heart attack, imaging report for stroke)
Specialist doctor's certificate confirming diagnosis meets policy definition
Survival period certificate (doctor's certificate confirming patient survived 30 days)
timeline_cashless_auth: N/A — lump sum, no hospitalization tracking
reimbursement_timeline: 30–45 days from receipt of complete documents. Lump sum paid once.
common_rejection_reasons: Diagnosis does not meet policy's specific definition (e.g., early-stage cancer not in covered list)
Pre-existing condition — illness diagnosed before policy inception
Waiting period not completed (usually 90 days initial waiting period for CI policies)
Survival period not met — policyholder died within 30 days of diagnosis
Non-disclosure of family history or previous symptoms at proposal stage
escalation_path: Insurer Grievance Cell → Independent Medical Expert Review → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman → Consumer Court

insurer: Star Health and Allied Insurance Co. Ltd.
title: Star Health — Cashless Hospitalization Claim
process: Step 1 — Find Star Health Network Hospital: Visit Star Health website or app, or call 1800-425-2255 to find a network hospital. Star Health has 14,000+ network hospitals across India.

Step 2 — Inform Star Health: For planned hospitalization, call the helpline at least 48 hours in advance. For emergencies, call within 24 hours of admission. Star Health manages claims in-house (no TPA involved).

Step 3 — Pre-authorization Request: The hospital's insurance desk submits a pre-auth request directly to Star Health's in-house claims team. Star Health responds within 1 hour (emergency) and 2–3 hours (planned).

Step 4 — Treatment and Discharge: Undergo treatment. At discharge, Star Health settles the approved amount directly with the hospital. You pay only copay, deductible (if any), and non-covered charges.

Step 5 — Document Collection: Even after cashless, collect discharge summary, final bills, lab reports — these are required if any post-discharge complication arises and you need to file a follow-up claim.

Step 6 — Post-discharge Consultation Claims: Claim post-hospitalization consultations (within 60 days) by submitting doctor bills and prescriptions to Star Health's nearest branch or via the online portal.
documents_required: Star Health insurance card (digital via app or physical)
tips: Star Health is an in-house TPA — no third-party intermediary. Decisions are faster.
Download the Star Health app — it has a hospital locator and digital insurance card.
For pre-auth delays beyond 2 hours for emergencies, escalate directly to the claims manager.
Pre-authorization approval is not a guarantee of full payment — final bill may result in adjustments for non-covered items.
common_rejection_reasons: PED waiting period not completed (3 years for standard plans)
Treatment classified as 'cosmetic' by the insurer
Non-medical expenses billed by hospital (toiletries, attendant charges, etc.)
Pre-authorization not obtained before treatment (except genuine emergencies)
escalation_path: Star Health Branch Manager → Star Health Grievance Officer (grievance@starhealth.in) → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman → Consumer Court

insurer: HDFC ERGO General Insurance Co. Ltd.
title: HDFC ERGO — Health Cashless Claim
process: Step 1 — Identify Network Hospital: Use HDFC ERGO app or website to find a cashless hospital. HDFC ERGO uses Medi Assist TPA for claims management.
Step 2 — Intimate Medi Assist TPA: Call Medi Assist helpline (1800-419-9898) or HDFC ERGO helpline (1800-2666) within 24 hours of emergency admission or 48 hours before planned hospitalization.
Step 3 — Pre-authorization: Hospital insurance desk contacts Medi Assist for pre-authorization. Medi Assist reviews and responds. Authorization letter is shared with the hospital.

Step 4 — Treatment and Discharge: Treatment proceeds. Hospital submits final bills to Medi Assist. TPA reviews and settles with the hospital. Policyholder pays only the deductible/non-covered amounts.
Step 5 — Deficiency Claims: If any expense is not covered in cashless, the policyholder can file a reimbursement for the balance within 30 days.
tips: Keep the Medi Assist TPA helpline number (1800-419-9898) saved separately — in emergencies, the hospital insurance desk may not know it.
HDFC ERGO Optima Restore and similar plans have restoration benefit — if SI gets exhausted, a fresh SI is activated. Track this with TPA.
For out-of-network hospital treatment, pay and file reimbursement — submit within 30 days of discharge.
common_rejection_reasons: Treatment at non-network hospital without emergency justification
Treatment classified as OPD procedure not requiring hospitalization
escalation_path: Medi Assist TPA → HDFC ERGO Grievance Cell (1800-2666 option 9) → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman

insurer: Bajaj Allianz General Insurance Co. Ltd.
title: Bajaj Allianz — Health Cashless Claim
process: Step 1 — Identify Bajaj Allianz Cashless Hospital: Use Bajaj Allianz website/app or call 1800-209-0144 for hospital network. Bajaj Allianz has 6,500+ network hospitals.

Step 2 — Intimate Bajaj Allianz: For emergencies, call within 24 hours. For planned admission, call 48–72 hours in advance. Bajaj Allianz processes cashless claims through in-house team and partner TPAs.

Step 3 — Pre-authorization Form: Hospital's insurance desk fills and submits the pre-auth form to Bajaj Allianz. Bajaj Allianz's claims team reviews and grants authorization within the IRDAI-mandated timeline.
Step 4 — Treatment and Discharge: Medical treatment is provided. Hospital submits final bills on discharge. Bajaj Allianz settles directly with hospital.
Step 5 — Final Settlement Confirmation: Bajaj Allianz sends a settlement notification to the policyholder showing approved amount, deductions, and any balance payable by the insured.
tips: Bajaj Allianz's Extra Care Plus plan has no room rent sub-limit — take advantage of this.
Use Bajaj Allianz's 'Caringly Yours' app for hospital search, pre-auth tracking, and claim status.
For senior citizen plans (Silver Health), copay applies — keep 50% of bill amount ready.
common_rejection_reasons: Admitted for observation without active treatment
Room rent sub-limit exceeded with proportionate deduction
Claim intimation delayed beyond policy requirement
escalation_path: Bajaj Allianz Branch → Grievance Cell (grievance@bajajallianz.co.in) → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

insurer: Niva Bupa Health Insurance Co. Ltd.
title: Niva Bupa — Reimbursement Claim
process: Step 1 — Treatment at Any Hospital: Get treated at any registered hospital (network or non-network). Niva Bupa's reimbursement facility covers non-network hospitals.
Step 2 — Intimate Niva Bupa Within 24 Hours: Call 1800-103-2529 within 24 hours of emergency hospitalization or 48 hours before planned admission.
Step 3 — Pay Bills and Collect Documents: Pay all bills and collect: discharge summary, itemized bills, all diagnostic reports, pharmacy receipts, referral letters.

Step 4 — Submit Claim Form: Download Niva Bupa's claim form from their website. Fill and submit with all documents via online portal or courier to Niva Bupa's claims address within 30 days of discharge.
Step 5 — Claims Processing: Niva Bupa processes claims within 30 days of receipt of complete documents. Their in-house claims team may request additional information within 7 working days.
Step 6 — Payment: Approved amount credited to the registered bank account (NEFT). Rejection must be communicated in writing with specific reason.
documents_required: All diagnostic reports (originals or insurer-certified copies)
tips: Niva Bupa's ReAssure 2.0 plan has 'Health Returns' — track this via their app even during reimbursement claims.
Niva Bupa has a 'Cashless Everywhere' feature (2024) — even non-network hospitals can sometimes process cashless for policyholders. Check if applicable.
For post-hospitalization claims (60 days), file a separate claim submission citing the original hospitalization claim number.
common_rejection_reasons: Documents incomplete — missing prescription-linked pharmacy bills
Minimum hospitalization of 24 hours not met
Hospital not registered or not in AYUSH list (for AYUSH claims)
escalation_path: Niva Bupa Customer Service → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman → Consumer Forum

insurer: Care Health Insurance (formerly Religare Health Insurance)
title: Care Health — Reimbursement Claim
process: Step 1 — Hospitalization and Payment: Get treated and pay all bills. Care Health's reimbursement covers hospitalization at any NABH/government-recognized hospital.
Step 2 — Claim Intimation: Call 1800-102-4488 within 24 hours of emergency admission. Provide policy number, patient name, and hospital name.
Step 3 — Document Collection: Before leaving hospital: discharge summary, itemized bill, all reports, pharmacy bills (with prescription), pre- and post-hospitalization documents.
Step 4 — Online Claim Filing: Go to Care Health's portal (careinsurance.com) → Claims → Reimbursement. Upload scanned copies. Physical documents to be mailed within 15 days of digital submission.
Step 5 — Query Response: Care Health may raise queries within 7 days. Respond within 15 days with requested documents.
Step 6 — Settlement: NEFT credit within 30 days of complete document submission.
tips: Care Health's 'Unlimited Restoration' feature: if SI is exhausted, fresh SI is available for different illness — document carefully.
Care Advantage plan covers OPD consultations — file these separately with prescription and bills.
Care Health has a 'Direct Claim' online portal with real-time status tracking.
common_rejection_reasons: Minimum 24-hour hospitalization not met for non-day-care procedures
Treatment not covered (dental, cosmetic, obesity treatment)
Hospital not NABH-accredited or not recognized
Claim intimation delay — beyond policy stipulated period
escalation_path: Care Health Branch → complaints@careinsurance.com → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman

title: LIC — Death Claim (Individual Life Policy)
process: Step 1 — Report Death to LIC Branch: Nominee (or any family member) informs the nearest LIC branch or the servicing branch (where policy is held) about the death. Provide policy bond number and details.
Step 2 — Claim Registration: Fill LIC Death Claim Form A (Claimant's Statement) available at branch or licindia.in. LIC issues a claim number and acknowledges within 3 working days.
Step 3 — Document Submission: Submit all required documents at the LIC branch. For claims above ₹50 lakh or early claims (within 3 years), additional scrutiny is standard.

Step 4 — Investigation (if required): LIC may appoint an investigator for claims under 3 years of policy inception or where cause of death requires verification. Investigation completed within 90 days.
Step 5 — Settlement: Non-investigated claims settled within 30 days. Investigated claims within 120 days. Payment to nominee's registered bank account via NEFT.
Step 6 — Annuity Conversion (if applicable): For some policies (Jeevan Shanti, Jeevan Akshay), the nominee may have an option to receive proceeds as annuity instead of lump sum.
documents_required: Death certificate issued by municipal corporation/gram panchayat
Medical certificate of cause of death
Post-mortem report (if accidental death or sudden death)
FIR and coroner's report (if accidental/unnatural death)
Photo ID and address proof of nominee
Legal evidence of title (if no nomination — succession certificate or legal heir certificate)
tips: LIC has 'Online Claim Submission' at licindia.in — faster than visiting branch for document submission.
If the original policy bond is lost, submit an Indemnity Bond (available at branch) along with stamp paper declaration.
LIC's 'IVRS' helpline (1800-33-5232) can guide nominees through the entire process.
For early claims (within 3 years), be prepared for investigator visit — cooperate fully and be ready with medical records.
common_rejection_reasons: Suicide within first year of policy (Section 45 — suicide in year 1 is excluded in most LIC policies)
Non-disclosure of material facts at proposal stage (challengeable within first 3 years)
Policy lapsed at time of death with no revival
Death under exclusion clause (war, nuclear peril)
Fraudulent claim — death certificate not genuine
escalation_path: LIC Branch Manager → LIC Divisional Office Claims Dept → LIC Zonal Office → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Life Insurance Ombudsman → Civil Court

title: HDFC Life — Death Claim
process: Step 1 — Intimate HDFC Life: Nominee calls 1860-267-9999 or emails claimsintimation@hdfclife.com to register the death claim. Claim is registered and a Claim Reference Number is issued.
Step 2 — Document Submission: Submit required documents at nearest HDFC Life branch or courier to HDFC Life's claims processing center.
Step 3 — Claims Assessment: HDFC Life's claims team reviews documents. For claims within 3 years of policy issue or revival, an investigation may be initiated.
Step 4 — Nominee Verification: HDFC Life verifies the nominee's identity and bank account details to prevent fraud.
Step 5 — Settlement: Non-investigated claims settled within 30 days of complete documents. HDFC Life's average claim settlement time is typically 7–10 working days for straightforward cases.
Step 6 — Confirmation Letter: Settlement letter sent to nominee confirming amount paid. Tax deduction certificate (Form 16A if TDS deducted) provided if applicable.
documents_required: Medical records if death due to illness
FIR and post-mortem report for accidental deaths
Cause of death certificate from treating doctor
tips: HDFC Life's CSR is 99%+ — one of the highest. Claims are usually processed within 7 working days.
Use HDFC Life's online portal for claim submission to speed up the process.
For group term claims through employer, the HR department coordinates with HDFC Life directly — nominees should contact HR first.
common_rejection_reasons: Policy lapsed and not revived before death
Death due to illegal activities or self-inflicted injury
Nominee not registered — legal heir certificate may be required
escalation_path: HDFC Life Branch → claimsgrievance@hdfclife.com → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Life Insurance Ombudsman → Civil Court

insurer: ICICI Lombard General Insurance Co. Ltd.
title: ICICI Lombard — Motor Own Damage Claim
process: Step 1 — Secure Scene and Call Helpline: After the accident, ensure safety. Call ICICI Lombard's motor claims helpline: 1800-2666 immediately. Register the claim and get a claim number.
Step 2 — Digital Intimation via IL Take Care App: Download the 'IL Take Care' app and register the claim digitally with photos. ICICI Lombard has AI-powered survey capabilities in the app.
Step 3 — Vehicle Survey: ICICI Lombard assigns a cashless surveyor for network garage claims or an independent surveyor for reimbursement claims. Surveyor inspects within 24–48 hours.

Step 4 — Cashless Repair at Network Garage: Take the vehicle to ICICI Lombard's 15,500+ cashless garages. Garage sends repair estimate to ICICI Lombard. After approval, repairs begin. Policyholder pays deductible.
Step 5 — Reimbursement at Non-Network Garage: Get repairs done at any garage. Collect all original bills. Submit to ICICI Lombard within 7 days of repair completion for reimbursement.
Step 6 — Settlement: ICICI Lombard releases payment within 7 working days of claim approval for cashless (direct to garage) or 15 working days for reimbursement.
documents_required: FIR (if theft or accident with injury/death)
Photos of damaged vehicle at accident site
Repair bills and GST invoices (for reimbursement)
tips: ICICI Lombard's AI survey via IL Take Care app can process minor claims without a physical surveyor visit — faster settlement.
Do NOT get vehicle repaired before surveyror inspection, except for emergency safety repairs.
NCB protection add-on: if you have it, filing one claim per year does not reduce your NCB at renewal.
Spot cashless settlement of up to ₹50,000 is possible at ICICI Lombard's preferred garages.
common_rejection_reasons: Driving under influence of alcohol or drugs
Driving license expired at time of accident
Vehicle used for commercial purpose without commercial insurance
Delay in intimation beyond policy requirement
Damage inconsistent with reported accident (suspected fraud)
escalation_path: ICICI Lombard Claims Team → Grievance Cell (1800-2666) → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Motor Insurance Ombudsman → Consumer Forum

insurer: The New India Assurance Co. Ltd.
title: New India Assurance — Motor Own Damage Claim
process: Step 1 — Intimate New India Branch: Call 1800-209-1415 or visit the nearest New India Assurance branch with the vehicle details. Register the claim.
Step 2 — Surveyor Appointment: New India appoints a government-licensed independent surveyor. For vehicles in remote areas, a 'spot survey' may be conducted via photos.
Step 3 — Survey and Report: Surveyor inspects damage and submits a survey report to New India, including the recommended repair scope and estimated amount.

Step 4 — Repair Authorization: New India authorizes repairs at a network garage or issues a survey report for a non-network garage. For cashless repairs, authorization goes directly to the network garage.
Step 5 — Claim Settlement: After repairs, the garage submits bills. New India settles directly (cashless) or reimburses the policyholder within 30 days of complete documents.
Step 6 — Discharge Voucher: For final settlement, New India may require a signed discharge voucher confirming the policyholder's acceptance of the settlement amount.
tips: New India Assurance is a government PSU insurer — branch-based process may take longer than private insurers.
Follow up regularly with the branch claims officer for survey report status.
New India has a wider rural branch network than most private insurers — useful for rural vehicle claims.
Claim settlement authority limits: small claims (< ₹50,000) settled at branch level; larger claims go to divisional/regional office.
common_rejection_reasons: Policy not in force at time of accident (non-renewal)
Driver license invalid (not a valid class for the vehicle type)
Flood damage without flood/engine protect endorsement
Theft not reported to police within 24 hours
escalation_path: Branch Claims Officer → Divisional Manager → Regional Manager → New India HO Grievance → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Motor Third-Party Injury/Death Claim at MACT
process: Step 1 — FIR is Mandatory: File FIR at the nearest police station with vehicle number of the at-fault vehicle. FIR is the foundation of the TP claim.
Step 2 — Medical Treatment: Victim (or family of deceased) should get immediate medical treatment. MACT can order interim compensation for medical expenses even before final award.
Step 3 — Consult a Lawyer: TP claims go to Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT — district level court). File an application (petition) through a lawyer. No court fee is payable.
Step 4 — MACT Petition Filing: Petition includes: FIR details, description of accident, nature of injury/death, income proof of victim, details of at-fault vehicle's insurance.
Step 5 — Service and Hearing: MACT serves notice to the at-fault vehicle owner and their insurer. Insurance company files a counter-claim. Witnesses are examined. Takes 1–3 years typically.

Step 6 — Compensation Award: MACT calculates compensation using Sarla Verma formula (Supreme Court) for death: based on income, age, and dependency. For injury: medical expenses + loss of income + pain and suffering.
Step 7 — Payment: Insurer must pay within 30 days of award. Default attracts 9–12% interest per annum.
documents_required: Income proof of victim (salary slips, ITR, bank statements)
Identity proof of victim and claimant
Disability certificate (for disability claims — from authorized medical board)
tips: MACT Interim Compensation: For urgent medical bills, apply for interim compensation immediately after filing the petition — MACT can order advance payment within days.
Hit-and-run with unknown vehicle: claim from Solatium Fund — ₹2 lakh for death, ₹50,000 for grievous injury. File at district collector's office.
Claimant does not need to prove whose fault it was in most cases — 'no fault liability' applies for third parties under Motor Vehicles Act.
Compensation formula: Sarla Verma (death) = monthly income × 12 × multiplier (based on age) × 2/3 dependency ratio.
common_rejection_reasons: FIR not filed — MACT may not proceed without FIR
Insurer contests policy validity (lapsed policy, unlicensed driver)
Claim filed beyond 3 years from accident (limitation period)
Vehicle not insured — insurer can be excluded, claim may go against owner personally
escalation_path: MACT → High Court (appeal within 90 days of MACT order) → Supreme Court (for substantial legal questions)

title: Travel Insurance — Medical Emergency Abroad Claim
process: Step 1 — Call Emergency Assistance: Most travel policies include 24/7 emergency assistance via a toll-free or reverse-charge number provided in the policy card. Call immediately on any medical emergency abroad. The assistance company coordinates hospitalization and cashless facility.

Step 2 — Cashless Treatment Abroad: For serious emergencies, the assistance company can direct guarantee of payment (GOP) to the overseas hospital — enabling cashless treatment. This avoids out-of-pocket payment for large bills.
Step 3 — Pay and Claim (For Minor Bills): For minor emergencies (clinic visits, pharmacy), pay from pocket and file reimbursement on return. Keep all bills, receipts, and medical reports in original.

Step 4 — Claim Filing on Return: File the travel insurance claim within 30 days of return. Submit via insurer's portal or send physical documents. Most travel insurers have dedicated international claims teams.
Step 5 — Document Submission: Provide all medical documentation, bills, proof of travel dates, and emergency assistance reference number (if used).
Step 6 — Settlement: Travel insurance claims are typically settled within 15–30 days of complete document submission. Payments may be in INR or USD depending on policy terms.
documents_required: Itemized hospital bills and payment receipts
tips: CRITICAL: Call the emergency assistance number first — don't just walk into an overseas hospital and start accruing bills without coordinating with the assistance company.
Emergency assistance companies can arrange medical evacuation (repatriation) if needed — covered under most travel policies.
Pre-existing conditions are usually excluded from medical emergency cover abroad — do not assume travel insurance covers your diabetes hospitalization.
Multi-trip annual policies are cost-effective for frequent travelers — ₹8,000–₹15,000/year for unlimited trips (each trip up to 30–45 days).
common_rejection_reasons: Pre-existing disease — emergency hospitalization caused by a pre-existing condition
Adventure sports injury — not covered unless adventure sports rider is added
Drunk/intoxicated — injury while under influence excluded
Pregnancy complications abroad — usually excluded (unless within first trimester and emergency)
Medical tourism — planned treatment abroad is not covered
escalation_path: Travel Insurer's International Claims Team → Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman

title: Travel Insurance — Baggage Loss Claim
process: Step 1 — Report to Airline Immediately: File a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) at the airline's lost baggage counter at the destination airport. Get a PIR reference number — this is mandatory for the insurance claim.

Step 2 — Follow Up with Airline: Track your baggage via the airline's website using the PIR number. Airlines typically have 21 days to deliver delayed/lost baggage. After 21 days, baggage is officially declared 'lost'.
Step 3 — Intimate Travel Insurer: Call or email your travel insurer within 24–48 hours of discovering the baggage loss. Provide policy number, PIR reference, flight details.

Step 4 — Compile Replacement Receipts: For baggage loss, claim reimbursement for essential replacement purchases. Keep all receipts. For delayed baggage (different from lost), claim for emergency purchases during delay period.
Step 5 — Claim Submission: Submit claim form with PIR, airline's confirmation of loss/damage, list of lost items (with approximate value), replacement purchase receipts, and boarding pass.
Step 6 — Settlement: Travel insurer settles based on policy's baggage loss limit (usually ₹50,000–₹2 lakh per person for international travel).
documents_required: Airline's confirmation of loss (after 21 days for permanent loss)
List of lost items with estimated values
Purchase receipts for essential replacement items
tips: Valuable items (jewellery, electronics) are often excluded or have low sub-limits in travel insurance — declare and insure separately if carrying expensive items.
Baggage delay cover (separate from baggage loss) kicks in if baggage arrives 6–12 hours late — covers emergency purchases.
Airlines have a limited liability (Warsaw Convention or Montreal Convention) for baggage loss — travel insurance covers the excess above airline liability.
For international claims, submit in the currency used (USD/EUR) if you have receipts — insurer converts to INR at prevailing rate.
common_rejection_reasons: No PIR filed at airport — airlines' confirmation is mandatory
Valuable items not covered under policy
Baggage left unattended (theft excluded if left unattended)
Sports equipment excluded from standard baggage cover
escalation_path: Travel Insurer Claims Team → Insurer Grievance → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Domiciliary Hospitalization Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Doctor Certificate of Necessity: Obtain a written certificate from the treating doctor stating: (a) the condition requires hospitalization-level care, (b) hospitalization is not possible due to non-availability of hospital beds or the patient's condition prevents transfer, (c) treatment will be administered at home.

Step 2 — Intimate Insurer Immediately: Call TPA/insurer as soon as domiciliary treatment begins. Most policies require notification within 24–48 hours of starting domiciliary treatment. Without prior intimation, reimbursement may be rejected.

Step 3 — Maintain Treatment Records: Keep detailed records of all home visits by doctor/nurse, all medicines administered, all nursing/caregiver bills, and any medical equipment rented (oxygen concentrator, suction machine, etc.).
Step 4 — Treatment Duration: Most policies require minimum 3 days of domiciliary treatment for a claim to be valid.
Step 5 — Submit Claim: After treatment concludes, file reimbursement claim with all documents. Some insurers require a 'Domiciliary Claim Form' — check your insurer's website.
Step 6 — Settlement: Reimbursement within 30 days of complete document submission.
documents_required: Doctor/nurse visit bills with date, time, and signature
Any pathology/lab bills for home-collected samples
tips: Domiciliary claims are frequently disputed — ensure the doctor's certificate is very specific about why hospitalization is not possible.
Common conditions for domiciliary: elderly patient unable to travel, no ICU beds available, infectious disease (COVID, typhoid), severe arthritis preventing movement.
Not all treatments qualify — some policies list specific conditions covered for domiciliary treatment.
Maintain a daily treatment log with entry/exit times of medical staff — prevents claims from being questioned.
common_rejection_reasons: Doctor's certificate vague or not specifying reason hospitalization was not possible
Treatment for chronic condition (e.g., routine insulin administration) not covered under domiciliary
Intimation not given before starting treatment
Condition not on insurer's approved domiciliary conditions list
escalation_path: TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Day Care Procedure Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Confirm Day Care Coverage: Verify that the planned procedure is on the insurer's Day Care Treatment list (IRDAI mandates all insurers cover 540+ day care procedures). Common day care: cataract, chemotherapy, dialysis, lithotripsy, tonsillectomy, hernia under local anesthesia, endoscopy.
Step 2 — Network Hospital for Cashless: For cashless, go to a network hospital with day surgery facilities. Inform TPA 48 hours in advance (for planned procedures).
Step 3 — Pre-authorization (for Cashless): Hospital insurance desk files pre-auth request with TPA. TPA approves — cashless available for the day care procedure.

Step 4 — Treatment and Discharge (Same Day): Day care procedures are completed and patient discharged within the same working day. Minimum 24-hour hospitalization requirement is WAIVED for day care procedures.
Step 5 — Document Collection: Even for cashless, collect discharge summary with procedure name and ICD code, anesthesia record (if used), and pharmacy bills.
Step 6 — Reimbursement (if Non-cashless): File reimbursement within 30 days with: procedure receipt, day surgery admission and discharge summary, doctor's notes, pharmacy bills.
documents_required: Pre and post procedure prescriptions and bills
tips: Day care claim does NOT require 24-hour hospitalization — this is explicitly covered by IRDAI for approved procedures.
If the insurer denies a day care claim citing 'less than 24 hours hospitalization', this is against IRDAI guidelines — escalate immediately.
Dialysis sessions (if needed multiple times a week) are day care — each session is a separate day care claim entry.
Chemotherapy day care: each treatment cycle is a separate day care claim — keep session summaries.
common_rejection_reasons: Procedure not on insurer's approved day care list (rare — IRDAI list is comprehensive)
Procedure done in an unrecognized facility
No anesthesia administered — insurer may question if 'hospitalization' was necessary
PED waiting period not served for the condition being treated
escalation_path: TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance Cell → IRDAI (cite IRDAI Day Care guidelines) → Ombudsman

title: Pre-Authorization Process for Planned Surgery
process: Step 1 — Doctor's Recommendation: Obtain doctor's prescription/referral recommending the surgery or procedure. This forms the basis of the pre-authorization request.

Step 2 — Hospital Insurance Desk: Visit the hospital's insurance desk (at the cashless network hospital) with your health insurance card, photo ID, and doctor's prescription. The insurance desk handles the pre-authorization process.

Step 3 — Pre-auth Form Submission: The hospital fills the insurer's/TPA's pre-authorization form — including: patient demographics, policy details, diagnosis, proposed treatment/procedure, expected admission date, estimated cost.

Step 4 — TPA/Insurer Review: TPA reviews the request against policy terms: Is the condition covered? Is the waiting period served? Is the estimated cost within reasonable range? TPA may request additional reports (e.g., surgeon's notes, recent ECG).

Step 5 — Authorization Decision: Within 1 hour (emergency) or 4 hours (planned) as per IRDAI norms, TPA issues: full approval (proceed cashless), partial approval (with conditions), or denial (with reasons).

Step 6 — Proceed with Treatment: On approval, the hospital proceeds with admission and treatment. The authorization letter specifies the approved amount — final claim may adjust based on actual bills.
documents_required: Recent diagnostic reports supporting the diagnosis
tips: Request a pre-authorization 2–3 weeks before the planned surgery — allows time for any additional document requirements.
Keep a copy of the authorization letter and reference number — it is proof of cashless approval.
Pre-authorization can be denied for various reasons — if denied, you can appeal with additional medical justification.
For organ transplants or very expensive procedures, insurers may require a second medical opinion.
common_rejection_reasons: PED waiting period not completed for the diagnosed condition
Insufficient medical evidence (no supporting diagnostic reports)
Procedure classified as elective/cosmetic by insurer
Estimated cost significantly exceeds benchmark for the procedure (insurer may request revised estimate)
escalation_path: TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance → IRDAI Bima Bharosa (for pre-auth delays exceeding IRDAI mandated timeline) → Ombudsman

title: LIC — Maturity Claim (Endowment / Money Back)
process: Step 1 — Pre-maturity Notification from LIC: LIC sends a maturity notice 2–3 months before the policy maturity date to the registered address. Check your address is updated in LIC's records.

Step 2 — Download/Collect Discharge Form: LIC sends a Discharge Voucher. Sign the form in the presence of a witness (both signatures required). For amounts above ₹50,000, the original policy bond must be surrendered.
Step 3 — KYC Submission: Submit PAN card (mandatory for amounts above ₹50,000 as per Income Tax), Aadhaar, and NEFT mandate with cancelled cheque.
Step 4 — Submit at LIC Branch: Submit at the servicing branch (where the policy is held) or online at licindia.in for digital submission.

Step 5 — Tax Assessment: LIC checks if maturity is taxable under Section 10(10D). If premium/SA ratio does not qualify for exemption, LIC deducts TDS at 5% on the maturity gain. Submit Form 15G/15H (if income below taxable limit) to avoid TDS.
Step 6 — Payment: LIC credits the maturity amount to the NEFT account within 15–30 days of document submission.
tips: Update your mobile number, email, and bank account at LIC well before maturity.
If the policy bond is lost, submit a Bond Surrender Indemnity (BSI) on stamp paper as a substitute.
For money back plans, survival benefits already paid reduce the final maturity proceeds — LIC statement shows all previously paid amounts.
If you have a loan against the policy, LIC will deduct outstanding loan + interest from maturity proceeds before paying the balance.
common_rejection_reasons: Bank account details mismatch — wrong IFSC or account number
PAN not submitted (TDS not deductible — LIC delays payment)
Policy bond not surrendered for large amounts
escalation_path: LIC Branch → Divisional Claims → LIC HO → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Life Insurance Ombudsman

insurer: All Health Insurers — IRDAI Portability Rules
title: Health Insurance Portability Process
process: Step 1 — Initiate Before Renewal: Start the portability process 45–60 days before your current policy's renewal date. IRDAI requires you to apply at least 45 days before expiry.
Step 2 — Choose New Insurer: Research plans, compare premiums, and select the new insurer. Ensure the new plan covers at least what the old plan covered.

Step 3 — Submit Portability Application: Fill the new insurer's portability application form. Submit it to the new insurer along with: current policy document, last 3 years' claim history, proposal form.

Step 4 — New Insurer Contacts Old Insurer: The new insurer requests your policy and claim history from the old insurer through IRDAI's portability portal. Old insurer must respond within 7 working days.

Step 5 — Underwriting Decision: New insurer reviews your portability file. They must accept portability for the sum insured you had with the old insurer (same or lower) — they CANNOT refuse based on portability alone. For a higher SI, fresh underwriting applies.

Step 6 — Policy Issuance: New insurer issues the policy continuing all waiting period credits from the old insurer. The old policy should be allowed to lapse without renewal fees (since you are porting).
Step 7 — Confirm Continuity: Verify that the new policy explicitly states the credit for waiting periods served (PED, specific disease waiting periods) — get this in writing.
documents_required: Medical records if any PED is listed
Bank account details for premium payment
tips: Compare ICR (Incurred Claim Ratio) and CSR of the new insurer before porting.
During portability, network hospitals and TPA may change — verify network hospitals near your home.
Portability preserves waiting period credit — if you have served 3 years of PED waiting period with the old insurer, the new insurer must credit those 3 years.
You CANNOT port during the policy year — only at renewal.
common_rejection_reasons: Portability application filed after the 45-day window
New insurer refuses portability (illegal under IRDAI — escalate to IRDAI immediately)
Requested SI is higher than what the old policy provided — new insurer can underwrite the difference
Old insurer does not provide claim history within 7 days (file complaint with IRDAI)
escalation_path: New Insurer Underwriting Team → IRDAI (if portability is illegally refused) → Insurance Ombudsman

insurer: All Critical Illness Insurers — Cancer Focus
title: Critical Illness Claim — Cancer Diagnosis
process: Step 1 — Obtain Pathology Report: For cancer CI claim, a biopsy pathology report confirming cancer diagnosis is the primary requirement. The report must be from an NABH-accredited lab or government-recognized pathology lab.

Step 2 — Oncologist Certificate: Get a certificate from a qualified oncologist (MBBS + MD/DNB in Oncology) confirming: cancer type, stage, ICD-10 code, date of diagnosis, and that the diagnosis meets the policy's definition of 'cancer of specified severity.'

Step 3 — Survival Period Verification: Most CI policies require surviving 30 days after diagnosis. If the policyholder dies within 30 days of cancer diagnosis, the CI claim may be rejected (though the life insurance death claim applies).
Step 4 — Intimate Insurer: Call insurer within 7–15 days of diagnosis. Provide: policy number, diagnosis details, treating hospital and oncologist details.
Step 5 — Independent Medical Examiner (IME): Insurer may appoint an IME to verify the diagnosis and confirm it meets the policy definition. IME report is shared with the claimant.
Step 6 — Lump Sum Payout: On approval, the CI benefit (₹10–₹1 crore depending on policy) is paid directly to the policyholder's bank account. No bills required — it is a defined benefit payout.
documents_required: Imaging reports (CT scan, PET scan, MRI)
tips: IRDAI standardizes CI definitions — 'cancer of specified severity' excludes: skin cancers (other than melanoma), prostate cancer Gleason score < 6, early-stage cancers (carcinoma in situ). Confirm your diagnosis meets the definition.
Some CI policies cover specific early-stage cancers at a reduced benefit (20–25% of SA). Check your policy.
CI benefit is a defined benefit — it is tax-free under Section 10(10D) when received as a rider benefit (subject to conditions).
After receiving CI benefit, the rider benefit is exhausted. The base life policy continues if it is a separate product.
common_rejection_reasons: Diagnosis does not meet IRDAI's standard definition of 'cancer of specified severity'
Early-stage cancer (carcinoma in situ) not covered under standard CI
Initial waiting period not completed (usually 90 days for CI policies)
30-day survival period not met (policyholder died within 30 days of diagnosis)
PED — cancer diagnosed before policy inception
escalation_path: Insurer Claims Department → Independent Medical Board Review → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Consumer Forum (for expert medical testimony disputes)

title: Group Health Insurance (Employer) — Claim Process
process: Step 1 — Contact HR Department: For group health insurance claims, the first point of contact is the HR/admin department of your employer. They coordinate with the insurer/TPA and can guide you on the specific process for your company's group policy.

Step 2 — Cashless at Network Hospital: For planned hospitalization, check the insurer's hospital network. Contact the hospital's insurance desk with your group health card (or employee ID). The insurance desk contacts the TPA.
Step 3 — TPA Pre-authorization: TPA issues pre-authorization for cashless. Many employers have dedicated TPA contact numbers for their employees — use this for faster service.

Step 4 — Discharge and Post-treatment Documents: After discharge, collect all original documents. Some group policies cover family members — check if the claim is for employee, spouse, children, or parents.
Step 5 — Reimbursement Submission: For non-cashless claims, submit to HR or directly to TPA with the claim form. HR typically has a deadline for submitting claim forms (usually 30 days).
Step 6 — Settlement: TPA processes and settles to employee's bank account (usually deducted/credited via payroll or direct NEFT).
documents_required: Completed claim form (employer-specific format or TPA format)
tips: Check if your employer's group policy covers parents and in-laws — not all do. Pre-authorization for parents may require additional employer approval.
Group policies often have no PED waiting period — coverage from day 1 for all conditions. Verify this advantage with HR.
If you resign/retire, group coverage ends — immediately buy an individual health policy. Portability from group to individual is possible via IRDAI rules.
Keep a copy of the master policy document from HR — it has the exact list of covered procedures, sub-limits, and exclusions.
common_rejection_reasons: Employee relationship terminated before claim date
Dependent not registered in the group policy (e.g., child born after policy inception not added)
Treatment date falls during a lapse period (if group policy premium unpaid by employer)
Sub-limits exceeded (room rent, maternity sub-limit in group policy)
escalation_path: HR Department → TPA Helpline → Insurer Grievance → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Life Insurance Policy Surrender (Voluntary Termination)
process: Step 1 — Check Eligibility: Surrender is available only after a minimum premium-paying period (typically 2–3 years for traditional policies, 5 years for ULIPs after lock-in). Calculate surrender value before proceeding.

Step 2 — Obtain Surrender Value Quote: Call the insurer's helpline or visit the branch to get the exact surrender value calculation — Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) vs Special Surrender Value (SSV). Note the amount before committing.
Step 3 — Submit Surrender Request Form: Fill the surrender form at the insurer's branch or online. For amounts above ₹50,000, the original policy bond must be submitted.
Step 4 — KYC and Bank Details: Submit: PAN card, Aadhaar, NEFT mandate, cancelled cheque. Ensure bank account is in the policyholder's name.
Step 5 — Tax Assessment: Insurer calculates TDS liability. For policies not qualifying under Section 10(10D), TDS at 5% (with PAN) or 20% (without PAN) applies on the gain amount.
Step 6 — Payment: Surrender value credited to NEFT account within 7–15 working days of document submission.
tips: Compare surrender value vs paid-up conversion — surrendering may be suboptimal if the policy has many years left to maturity.
For ULIPs within lock-in period: surrender value goes into discontinued policy fund (4% p.a.) — paid only after 5 years from inception.
If the reason for surrender is premium unaffordability, consider making the policy paid-up instead.
Check for any outstanding loan against the policy — surrender value will be netted after loan repayment.
common_rejection_reasons: Minimum premium-paying period not completed (below GSV eligibility)
Policy under assignment — assignee's NOC required
escalation_path: Insurer Branch → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Ombudsman

title: Motor Insurance — Vehicle Theft Claim
process: Step 1 — FIR is Mandatory: File FIR at the nearest police station within 24 hours of discovering theft. This is non-negotiable — no FIR, no claim.
Step 2 — Intimate Insurer Immediately: Call the insurer's helpline immediately after filing FIR. Provide: policy number, vehicle RC number, FIR details. Time is critical — delay can void the claim.
Step 3 — RTO Intimation: Inform the Regional Transport Office (RTO) about the vehicle theft. The RTO issues an 'RC Cancellation Certificate' once the vehicle is confirmed stolen.

Step 4 — Non-traceable Certificate (NTC): Wait for police investigation. If the vehicle is not recovered within 90 days, police issue a Non-Traceable Certificate (also called Final Report or NTC). This is required for claim settlement.
Step 5 — Submit Claim with NTC: Submit all documents to the insurer after receiving NTC. Insurer arranges final survey and settlement based on IDV (less depreciation and policy deductible).
Step 6 — Sign Form 35 (Hypothecation Release): If vehicle has a loan (hypothecation with bank), get Form 35 signed by the bank releasing their claim on the vehicle before insurer can pay.
documents_required: Original RC book to be surrendered
All original keys of the vehicle (both sets)
RTO transfer application and RC cancellation
Form 35 (hypothecation NOC from bank if applicable)
tips: Vehicle tracking: if your vehicle has GPS/telematics installed, share the location history with the insurer — can help in recovery.
Duplicate keys: insurers will ask for all key sets — missing a key set raises suspicion of insurance fraud.
During the 90-day waiting period, follow up actively with police for recovery attempts.
If vehicle is recovered but damaged, switch to OD claim — theft + recovery with damage is an OD claim not a full theft claim.
common_rejection_reasons: FIR filed after 24-hour delay without valid reason
Claim intimation delayed significantly after FIR
All keys not submitted (suspicion of inside job)
Policy lapsed at time of theft
Non-Traceable Certificate not obtained from police
escalation_path: Insurer Claims Manager → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Motor Insurance Ombudsman → Civil Court

title: Travel Insurance — Trip Cancellation Claim
process: Step 1 — Cancel Trip with Service Providers: Cancel flights, hotels, and tour packages immediately with the airlines and hotels. Get cancellation confirmations and refund amount statements from each service provider.

Step 2 — Doctor Certificate (if Medical Reason): If cancellation is due to illness or injury, obtain a medical certificate from a registered doctor stating: patient's diagnosis, severity, and explicit recommendation against travel.
Step 3 — Intimate Insurer Immediately: Call travel insurer as soon as the decision to cancel is made — before the trip departure date. Late intimation (after the trip date) is a ground for rejection.

Step 4 — Claim Filing: Submit trip cancellation claim form with: cancellation confirmation from all service providers, refund amounts received (or refund rejection confirmation), total non-refundable amount.
Step 5 — Document Compilation: Attach: booking invoices, cancellation confirmation emails, refund statements, medical certificate (if applicable), travel policy.
Step 6 — Settlement: Insurer pays the non-refundable, forfeited portion of the trip cost up to the policy's trip cancellation limit.
documents_required: Refund statements (showing what was refunded and what was forfeited)
Death certificate (if trip cancelled due to death in family)
tips: Cancel as early as possible — many hotels and airlines have tiered cancellation fees. Earlier cancellation = smaller loss.
Keep all email correspondence with airlines and hotels — these prove the cancellation and non-refundable amount.
Know covered reasons: illness, death in family, natural disaster at destination, job loss (some policies), visa denial. Personal preference to cancel is NOT covered.
CFAR (Cancel For Any Reason) travel insurance is available at higher premium — covers cancellation for any reason (not common in India).
common_rejection_reasons: Cancellation due to personal preference or change of mind — not a covered reason
Insurer not intimated before trip departure date
Medical certificate from non-qualified doctor or incomplete certificate
Trip cost can be fully recovered from airline/hotel refund — insurer pays only non-refundable portion
Flight cancelled by airline (airline liability, not travel insurance — airline must refund)
escalation_path: Travel Insurer Claims → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Insurance Ombudsman

title: Personal Accident Insurance — Disability Claim (PTD/PPD)
process: Step 1 — Medical Treatment and Stabilization: Get immediate treatment. For PA disability claims, the claim can only be filed after the condition is declared permanent — not during acute treatment phase.

Step 2 — Wait for Stabilization (180 days): Most PA policies require a waiting/stabilization period (typically 180 days or 6 months) before a disability is declared permanent. File the claim after this period.

Step 3 — Medical Board Certificate: Obtain a Permanent Disability Certificate from a government-recognized medical board (e.g., State Medical Board, AIIMS Medical Board) or from a specialist approved by the insurer. The certificate must specify: nature of disability, percentage of disability, and permanence.
Step 4 — Intimate Insurer: File the PA disability claim form with the insurer — provide FIR, accident details, medical treatment history, and disability certificate.
Step 5 — Independent Medical Examiner: The insurer may appoint their own IME to verify the disability percentage. IME report is considered alongside the medical board certificate.
Step 6 — Lump Sum Payment: Insurer pays: for PTD = 100% of sum assured; for PPD = the applicable percentage per IRDAI's standard PA disability schedule × SA. Payment is tax-free.
documents_required: Medical treatment records from date of accident
tips: Do not rush the disability certificate — the condition must be truly permanent (at least 6 months of treatment/stabilization) before filing.
Know the IRDAI disability schedule — it lists exactly what percentage applies to each body part disability.
Occupational hazard exclusions: if the accident occurred during a hazardous activity not declared in the proposal form, the claim may be rejected.
PA benefit is over and above any health insurance — both can be claimed for the same accident.
common_rejection_reasons: Disability due to illness (not accident) — PA covers only accidents
Accident occurred during excluded activity (flying as non-professional passenger is usually covered; extreme sports may not be)
Claim filed before disability is declared permanent
Medical board certificate not from an approved/recognized authority
Intoxication at time of accident — excluded in most PA policies
escalation_path: PA Insurer Claims → Independent Medical Board → Grievance Cell → IRDAI Bima Bharosa → Consumer Court

title: Filing a Grievance with IRDAI Bima Bharosa
process: Step 1 — First, Exhaust Insurer Grievance Process: File a written complaint with the insurer's grievance officer first. The insurer has 15 days to acknowledge and 30 days to resolve. Keep all correspondence.
Step 2 — If Unresolved After 30 Days: If the insurer does not respond within 30 days, or gives an unsatisfactory response, escalate to IRDAI.

Step 3 — File on Bima Bharosa Portal: Visit the IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal. Register with your mobile number. File the complaint with: policy details, insurer name, grievance description, previous correspondence with insurer.
Step 4 — IRDAI Assigns Complaint to Insurer: IRDAI forwards the complaint to the concerned insurer's nodal officer. The insurer must respond to IRDAI within a stipulated time.
Step 5 — IRDAI Follow-up: IRDAI monitors insurer's response. If insurer's response is still unsatisfactory, IRDAI can direct the insurer to resolve.

Step 6 — Ombudsman Escalation: If IRDAI grievance is also unresolved, the next level is the Insurance Ombudsman — a quasi-judicial body that can issue binding awards up to ₹30 lakh for individual policyholders.
documents_required: Grievance letter previously sent to insurer
Insurer's response (or proof of no response)
All supporting documents for the claim
tips: Always complain in writing — emails, portal submissions with acknowledgment numbers are all valid.
Keep reference numbers for every interaction: claim number, grievance number, IRDAI complaint number.
Insurance Ombudsman is free, fast (typically 3–6 months for resolution), and binding for amounts up to ₹30 lakh.
Consumer Forum (NCDRC/state/district) is an alternative to Ombudsman — no monetary limit.
